rows.curriculumText:
Award Requirements

For 2024/25 intake and onwards, students are required to complete 31 credits for MSc in
Information Technology. In general, each subject takes place once a week in the
evening over a 13-week semester. Full-time students normally take four to five
subjects in a semester, whereas part-time students usually take two
subjects.  Some subjects might also be offered during the summer to
provide greater flexibility to students in designing their study pattern.

Students can choose to study a MScIT degree with stream(s) or without stream by taking the required subjects.

Programme Core subjects: P-core

Stream Core subjects: S-core

Awards

MScIT with Stream in Natural Language Processing

MScIT with Stream in Visual Computing

31

31

*Students can take Master level elective subjects within the MScIT programme to satisfy their elective requirements, subject to the pre-requisite and exclusion requirements.

** N o credit fee will be charged for the AIE subject.

 

List of Subjects

While every stream has its own group of core subjects, a range of electives is offered depending on the availability of teaching resources and the number of registered students. Students are allowed to choose from a common pool of electives within the Department of Computing, subject to vacancies available. All subjects are three-credit based unless otherwise stated.

#: Students could take up to 6 credits of Non-COMP electives offered by other Departments of PolyU during the study period.

All subjects are offered at the discretion of subject offering departments, depending on students’ demand and resources available.

Please also visit http://www.polyu.edu.hk/study for other details of the programme and admission.

Award Requirements

For 2024/25 intake and onwards, students are required to complete 31 credits for MSc in Blockchain Technology. In general, each subject takes place once a week in the evening over a 13-week semester. Full-time students normally take four to five subjects in a semester, whereas part-time students usually take two subjects. Some subjects might also be offered during the summer to provide greater flexibility to students in designing their study pattern.

Awards

MScBT

  With Dissertation

  6 Cores + 1 Elective* + 1 Dissertation + 1 AIE Subject**

  With Project

  6 Cores + 2 Electives* + 1 Project  + 1 AIE Subject**

  Without Dissertation/ Project

  6 Cores + 4 Electives*  + 1 AIE Subject**

  Credits requirements

 31

*Any 6 out of 8 core subjects should be chosen and students are allowed to take additional Core subjects to fulfil the Elective requirement.

**N o credit fee will be charged for the AIE subject.

Below is the table summarising the core and elective subjects. All subjects are three-credit based, unless otherwise stated. Subjects such as Dissertation and Project have different assessment requirements and require supervision arrangement. More details can be obtained from the departmental intranet, i.e. http://intranet.comp.polyu.edu.hk .

Award Requirements

Each subject consists of one class a week over a 13-week semester. In general, each subject takes place once a week in the evening
over a 13-week semester. Students are normally enrolled in 4-5 subjects in a
semester. Some subjects might be offered during the summer to provide students
with greater flexibility in designing their study pattern.

For 2024/25 intake and onwards, students are required to complete 31 credits for MSc in
Metaverse Technology. All subjects are worth 3 credits unless otherwise
specified. Each student needs to complete the following subjects:

12 credits of Metaverse Programme core (including Metaverse
Project I and II)

6 credits of Metaverse Technology core

6 credits of Metaverse Design & Ecology core

6 credits of Electives

1 credit of AIE subject ( N o credit fee will be charged for the AIE subject
"EEE5T03 Engineering Ethics and Academic Integrity")

All subjects are offered at the discretion of subject offering
departments, depending on students’ demand and resources available.

Please also visit http://www.polyu.edu.hk/study for other details of the programme and admission.

Award Requirements

Students are required to complete 31 credits for MSc in Cybersecurity, typically over 1 year (full-time) or 2 years (part-time). All subjects carry 3 credits, except for the Dissertation (9 credits) and Project (6 credits). Full-time students generally enroll in five subjects per semester, while part-time students take two to three subjects.

Awards

MScCYS

  With Dissertation

4 Core + 3 Disciplinary-specific Electives + 1 Dissertation + 1 AIE Subject

  With Project

4 Core + 3 Disciplinary-specific Electives + 1 Elective + 1 Project + 1 AIE Subject

  Without Dissertation/ Project

4 Core + 3 Disciplinary-specific Electives +

3 Electives + 1 AIE Subject

  Credits requirements

31

Award Requirements

Students are required to complete 31 credits for MSc in Agentic AI Systems, typically over 1 year (full-time) or 2 years (part-time). All subjects carry 3 credits, except for the Dissertation (9 credits) and Project (6 credits). Full-time students generally enroll in five subjects per semester, while part-time students take two to three subjects.

Awards

MScCYS

  With Dissertation

3 Core + 3 Disciplinary-specific Electives + 1 Elective + 1 Dissertation + 1 AIE Subject

  With Project
3 Core + 3 Disciplinary-specific Electives + 2 elective + 1 Project + 1 AIE Subject

  Without Dissertation/ Project

3 Core + 3 Disciplinary-specific Electives + 4 Electives + 1 AIE Subject

  Credits requirements

31
